title: Vietnamese Fresh Spring Rolls with Caramel Hangover Cure
description: A plant-based-friendly twist on traditional banh cuon, featuring a rich caramel sauce hangover cure. Each bite is a fresh, springtime delight.

ingredients.name: Filling
ingredients.notes: Mix of shredded carrots, cabbage, and lettuce, along with chopped cilantro and mint.

ingredients.name: Caramel Hangover Cure Sauce
ingredients.notes: Equal parts coconut sugar, water, and tapioca starch, simmered until syrupy.

instructions: Wrap gozleme wrappers around the filling mixture, forming a tight cylinder shape.
Soak the spring rolls in warm water for 5-7 minutes, or until they're slightly softened.
Heat the caramel hangover cure sauce in a small saucepan over medium heat, stirring occasionally.
Add the sesame seeds, grated ginger, sliced scallions, toasted sesame oil, lime juice, sliced fresh chilies, chopped fresh cilantro, and chopped fresh mint to the saucepan, stirring to combine.
Bring the sauce to a simmer and cook for 2-3 minutes, or until it thickens slightly.
Serve the spring rolls hot, drizzled with the caramel hangover cure sauce and additional sesame seeds if desired.
